summary: PR 2748 split the former `melee/lb/lb_0192.c` range at 0x8001955C, leaving `lb_0192.c` as a matching object through 0x8001955C and moving the later functions, bss, and tail sdata2 range into new nonmatching `lb_0195.c`/`.h`. The PR updated splits, symbol ownership/scope, configure object status, and dependent includes from `lb_0192.h` to `lb_0195.h`. Bot output reports +812 linked code bytes, +1032 linked data bytes, and 14 new matches under the new `lb_0195` unit, while the same functions are reported as broken under the old `lb_0192` unit due to the split.

key_files.role: Moved the `lb_0192.c` text end from 0x80019BB8 to 0x8001955C and introduced the new `lb_0195.c` split covering text 0x8001955C-0x80019BB8, bss 0x804329F0-0x80432A68, and sdata2 0x804D7CA0-0x804D7CB0.

Adjusted data symbol names, scopes, and sizes around the split, including localizing many `lb_803B...` and `lb_804D...` symbols, preserving explicit `jp_msg` and `us_msg`, and reducing `lb_804D63D0` to a local 4-byte sbss symbol.
Changed `melee/lb/lb_0192.c` from NonMatching to Matching and added `melee/lb/lb_0195.c` as NonMatching.
Removed the later 0x8001955C+ functions and the `lb_804329F0` bss struct from this TU, leaving earlier code and a local `HSD_Text* lb_804D63D0` in the shortened split.
New source file receiving the moved reset/pad/alarm functions from `lb_0192.c`, including `lb_8001955C`, `lb_80019628`, `lb_80019900`, and `lb_80019AAC`.
evidence_refs: text_corpus:decomp-dev bot new matches for main/melee/lb/lb_0195

key_files.role: Reduced header surface after the split; the excerpt shows many 0x8001955C+ prototypes removed from this header.
New header for the moved 0x8001955C+ declarations.

what_changed: Split `melee/lb/lb_0192.c` at text address 0x8001955C and created `melee/lb/lb_0195.c` for 0x8001955C-0x80019BB8.
Moved the `lb_804329F0` bss ownership and the 0x804D7CA0-0x804D7CB0 sdata2 tail out of `lb_0192.c` and into `lb_0195.c` in `splits.txt`.
Promoted `melee/lb/lb_0192.c` to `Object(Matching, ...)` in `configure.py` and added `melee/lb/lb_0195.c` as `Object(NonMatching, ...)`.
Moved or exposed the 0x8001955C+ function declarations through new `src/melee/lb/lb_0195.h`; dependent files now include `lb_0195.h` rather than `lb_0192.h`.
Adjusted symbol table ownership and scope for data around the split, converting many address-style globals to local `@NN` symbols and keeping named local aggregates `jp_msg` and `us_msg`.

Bot report shows the new `main/melee/lb/lb_0195` unit gained full matches for multiple moved functions and data items, while the same items disappeared from the old `main/melee/lb/lb_0192` unit as expected for a split.

smart_moves.title: Split first, then update configure status per object
smart_moves.text: The PR does not try to keep the whole old `lb_0192.c` object nonmatching; it narrows `lb_0192.c` to a matching range and isolates the remaining work in new nonmatching `lb_0195.c`.

smart_moves.title: Move header consumers with the functions
smart_moves.text: Callers that needed the 0x8001955C+ declarations were updated from `lb_0192.h` to `lb_0195.h`, keeping the shortened `lb_0192.h` from exporting moved symbols.

smart_moves.title: Treat split-induced old-unit regressions as relocation evidence, not necessarily code regressions
smart_moves.text: The bot lists 11 broken matches under old `main/melee/lb/lb_0192`, but also lists the same functions as new matches under `main/melee/lb/lb_0195`, indicating the report noise is caused by unit relocation.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For object split PRs, update `splits.txt`, `configure.py`, source/header boundaries, and dependent includes together; otherwise matching status and prototypes can describe different TU boundaries.

When a split moves functions to a new TU, bot reports may show old-unit broken matches and new-unit matches for the same symbols; interpret both sides before treating the broken-match table as a regression.

naming_conventions.name: lb_0195
naming_conventions.meaning: New TU/header stem chosen from the split address 0x8001955C.

naming_conventions.name: jp_msg / us_msg
naming_conventions.meaning: Named local message-table-like data symbols retained in `symbols.txt` while nearby strings were localized to `@NN` symbols.

naming_conventions.name: @NN local symbols
naming_conventions.meaning: The PR localizes many data/sdata/sdata2 symbols from address-style `lb_...` or `lbl_...` names to `@NN` local symbols when no semantic global name is shown in the loaded evidence.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Cut the object at a function boundary, 0x8001955C, to make the earlier `lb_0192.c` range fully matching while leaving the later range in a new nonmatching TU.
evidence_refs: text_corpus:decomp-dev bot linked code and new matches

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Reassign section ownership in the split: `lb_0192.c` keeps data 0x803BA708-0x803BAAE0, sdata 0x804D3800-0x804D3808, sbss 0x804D63D0-0x804D63D8, and sdata2 only through 0x804D7CA0; `lb_0195.c` takes bss and the tail sdata2.
Update symbol scopes to match new local ownership rather than keeping broad global address-style names for split-local data.

The loaded diff is truncated before showing the full new `lb_0195.c` and `lb_0195.h` contents, so moved declarations and source details are inferred from the removed `lb_0192.c` body, changed-file metadata, and bot report rather than fully visible added hunks.
Bot output provides matching/report evidence for the split, but no manual validation command output or reviewer rationale is present.
One-line changes in `lbaudio_ax.c`, `lbcardnew.c`, `lbdvd.c`, and `lbfile.c` are listed in changed-file metadata but their exact hunks are not shown in the loaded excerpt.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Interpret split PR bot reports by comparing old-unit losses with new-unit gains
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR 2748, the bot reported broken matches for moved functions under old `main/melee/lb/lb_0192` and new matches for the same functions under new `main/melee/lb/lb_0195`. For object split PRs, correlate both tables before classifying old-unit broken matches as true regressions.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Object split PRs need coordinated split, configure, header, and include updates
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2748 changed `splits.txt`, changed `lb_0192.c` to Matching in `configure.py`, added `lb_0195.c` as NonMatching, reduced `lb_0192.h`, added `lb_0195.h`, and updated consumers to include the new header. This is a concrete example of keeping TU boundaries and prototype ownership aligned during a split.

source_update_candidates.title: `lb_0192.c` split boundary from PR 2748
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2748 split `src/melee/lb/lb_0192.c` at text address 0x8001955C. After the split, `lb_0192.c` covers text 0x80019230-0x8001955C and is configured as Matching.
source_update_candidates.reason: Path-scoped split boundary and matching-status fact may help future work distinguish old `lb_0192` ownership from the new `lb_0195` unit.

source_update_candidates.title: `lb_0195.c` introduced as tail split of `lb_0192.c`
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2748 introduced `src/melee/lb/lb_0195.c`/`.h` for the 0x8001955C-0x80019BB8 tail of former `lb_0192.c`; it owns bss 0x804329F0-0x80432A68 and sdata2 0x804D7CA0-0x804D7CB0 and was added to configure as NonMatching.
source_update_candidates.reason: New path ownership fact is directly supported by split/config metadata and changed-file records.

source_update_candidates.title: Object split report interpretation
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding a standards or review note that object-split PRs can show apparent old-unit match regressions paired with new-unit gains for the same functions; reviewers should compare both before requesting regression repair.
source_update_candidates.reason: PR 2748 is a concrete example, but one PR alone may be too narrow for a global standard without curator comparison to other split PRs.

rejection_notes: Do not infer a semantic name for `lb_804329F0` or its fields from this PR; the visible moved code still uses unknown field names such as `x0`, `x38`, and `x48`.

Do not treat the retained or existing `#pragma dont_inline` around `lb_800192A8` as a new matching tactic introduced by this PR; the excerpt shows it remains, but not that PR 2748 added or justified it.
Do not promote the bot report as proof that `lb_80019628` fully matches in `lb_0195`; the report says it improved to 99.60%, not 100.00%.
